Ask the Institution

auto_awesome Institutional Synthesis

The Lineage of Scholasticism in Modern Digital Ethics

The transition from medieval Scholastic rigor to contemporary digital frameworks reveals a persistent architecture of categorization. While the Summa Theologica sought to reconcile faith and reason through dialectic structure, modern ethical AI protocols utilize remarkably similar hierarchical taxonomies to navigate the tension between utility and personhood.

Specifically, the concept of "Hylomorphism"—the theory that being is composed of matter and form—finds a curious echo in the separation of data (matter) and algorithm (form) within large-scale intelligence systems. This institutional inquiry suggests that we are not entering a new ethical era, but rather applying 13th-century logic to silicon and the physical stack.
